channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Today he is a high school football player. Soon he'll be a Buddhist lama in the Himalayas
item.description: ISANTI, Minn. (AP) — The young Buddhist lama sat on a throne near an altar decorated with flowers, fruits and golden statues of the Buddha, watching the celebrations of his 18th birthday in silence, with a faint smile.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greece to repay chunk of bailout debt early and touts recovery during anger over cost of living
item.description: Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis says Greece is set to make an early repayment of 5 billion euros or $5.3 billion in bailout-era debt in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK hits Iran with new sanctions for supplying missiles to Russia
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. government hit Iran with new sanctions Monday for sending ballistic missiles and other weapons to Russia to support the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hungarian official criticizes Biden decision allowing Ukraine to use US weapons to strike Russia
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary's foreign minister lashed out on Monday at U.S. President Joe Biden's decision to authorize Ukraine to use missiles supplied by Washington to strike deeper inside Russia, saying the move could serve to escalate the war and threaten the outbreak of a global conflict.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thune threatens International Criminal Court with sanctions if it doesn’t drop Netanyahu warrant for arrest
item.description: Sen. John Thune said he will go after the International Criminal Court with sanctions once the GOP take the majority in the Senate come January if the court does not drop its case against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters in Georgia's capital set up a tent camp on the main street and call for new elections
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Demonstrators in the capital of Georgia have set up tents on one of the city's main thoroughfares and vowed Monday to stay there around the clock to demand new parliamentary elections in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French farmers escalate protests against an EU-Mercosur trade deal and fear unfair competition
item.description: French farmers have escalated their protests against the EU-Mercosur trade agreement under negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Host Brazil focuses G20 summit on fighting hunger amid wars and Trump's return
item.description: Brazil is pushing for concerted action to alleviate hunger as it hosts a summit of the Group of 20 leading economies amid global uncertainty over two major wars and incoming U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes And Mauricio Savarese, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden has authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside Russia: Reports
item.description: President Biden authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike further into Russia, multiple reports said. The move comes as about 10,000 North Korean troops aid Russia in its war and expect to fight in Ukraine in the “next several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Bevy of immigration programs at risk under Trump mass deportation plan
item.description: Immigrants with humanitarian or temporary legal status in the United States are at risk of being rendered effectively undocumented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Tropical Storm Sara weakens to tropical depression after making landfall in Belize
item.description: POTRERILLOS, Honduras (AP) — Tropical Storm Sara on Sunday weakened to a tropical depression after making landfall in Belize, where forecasters expected heavy rain to cause flash flooding and mudslides.
item.dc:creator: MOISÉS CASTILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launched more than 200 missiles, drones at Ukrainian infrastructure: Zelensky
item.description: On Sunday, Russia launched a drone and missile attack on Ukraine in what’s being seen as the largest barrage in recent months, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Russia grinds deeper into Ukraine after 1,000 days of grueling war
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — When Russian tanks rolled into Ukraine in February 2022, the conventional wisdom was that the capital, Kyiv, would soon fall and the rest of the country wouldn’t last long against a much larger enemy. Instead, it was that narrative that quickly collapsed.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia has launched 120 missiles and 90 drones in a large-scale attack across Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Latest typhoon lashes the Philippines, causing tidal surges and displacing massive numbers of people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A powerful typhoon wrecked houses, caused towering tidal surges and forced hundreds of thousands of people to flee to emergency shelters as it cut across the northern Philippines on Sunday in the sixth major storm to hit the country in less than a month.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican president might be changing view on US as Trump win sends warning to ruling socialists
item.description: Following Trump’s victory, the Mexican government, led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, is considering reassessing some of its policies to navigate the anticipated challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knife attack at a vocational school in eastern China leaves 8 dead and 17 injured
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A stabbing attack at a vocational school in the eastern Chinese city of Wuxi on Saturday left eight people dead and 17 others injured, local police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Lebanon considers cease-fire plan while Israel launches new strikes
item.description: Lebanon is considering a U.S.-backed cease-fire plan while Israel launched new strikes in the area south of Beirut. Senior Lebanon officials are reviewing the proposal and it is based on the U.N. Security Council resolution 1701, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian troops train for trench warfare near France's WWI battlefields
item.description: Around 2,000 Ukrainian soldiers are training in France's eastern Marne region, where German and French armies battled each other during World War I.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists in Argentina unearth oldest tadpole fossil ever discovered
item.description: A search for dinosaur fossils unintentionally shifted into a discovery of the oldest tadpole ever discovered when a team exploring in Argentina came across the find.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Priest stabbed in the face during Mass as religion-based hate crime is on the rise worldwide
item.description: A priest stabbed in the face during Mass is one of the most recent cases of a religion-based hate crime, which is one the rise around the globe, though Jews are most often victims of the attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Welsh government advised to create dog-free zones to combat racism
item.description: A climate change group has submitted a suggestion to Welsh government officials to create dog-free zones in an effort to combat racism, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 dead, Supreme Court evacuated as explosions rattle Brazilian capital
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court was evacuated Wednesday following two explosions in its vicinity, stoking security concerns in the G20's leadup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver rams his car into crowd in China, killing 35. Police say he was upset about his divorce
item.description: ZHUHAI, China (AP) — A man who authorities said was upset over his divorce settlement rammed his car into a crowd of people exercising at a sports complex in southern China, killing 35 and severely injuring dozens of others, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: NG HAN GUAN and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New search for MH370 Malaysia Airlines flight could begin soon
item.description: Flight MH370 vanished on March 8, 2014, shortly after departing Kuala Lumpur for Beijing with 239 people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: US says it will not limit Israel arms transfers after some improvements in flow of aid to Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that Israel has made some good but limited progress in increasing the flow of humanitarian aid to Gaza and will not limit arms transfers to Israel as it had threatened to a month ago if the situation had not improved.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Church of England head Justin Welby resigns over handling of sex abuse scandal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Archbishop of Canterbury Justin Welby, head of the Church of England and spiritual leader of the global Anglican Communion, resigned Tuesday after an investigation found that he failed to tell police about serial physical and sexual abuse by a volunteer at Christian summer camps as soo
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation
